info.title: Rendobar API
info.description: Serverless media processing API. Watermark, transcode, caption, render videos with one API call. Credit-based billing. MCP-native for AI agents.

tags.name: Watermark
tags.description: Add, remove, or detect watermarks in videos

tags.name: Captions
tags.description: Extract speech-to-text captions or burn subtitles into video

tags.name: Compliance
tags.description: Content moderation and GDPR PII detection/redaction

tags.name: Extract
tags.description: Extract metadata, detect scenes, or OCR text from video

tags.name: Transcode
tags.description: Convert video codec, resolution, or format

tags.name: FFmpeg
tags.description: FFmpeg-as-a-Service: probe media files and execute FFmpeg commands

tags.name: QR
tags.description: Generate styled QR codes with 9 content types (URL, vCard, WiFi, etc.)

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description: Generic job creation and management (list, get, cancel, download)

tags.name: Sharing
tags.description: Create and manage public share links for job outputs

tags.name: Batches
tags.description: Submit and manage batch job groups

tags.name: Pipelines
tags.description: Compose multi-job DAG pipelines with fan-out/fan-in execution

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: Check account balance and usage history

tags.name: Assets
tags.description: Upload and manage PSD templates and design assets

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Real-time job status updates via SSE

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Configure webhook endpoints and view delivery history

post.summary: Apply watermarks to video or image
post.description: Overlay one or more watermarks (images and/or text) onto a video or image. Supports 9-position grid, rotation, shadow, outline, opacity, quality presets, and time-ranged visibility (video only). Up to 10 watermarks per job.

source.description: URL to the source video or image file

url.description: URL to the watermark image file

timeRange.description: Time range for watermark visibility (video only)

fade.description: Fade in/out effect for the watermark

content.description: Text content to render as watermark

fontUrl.description: URL to a custom font file (must use HTTPS)

shadow.description: Text shadow (true for defaults, or object with color/offset)

outline.description: Text outline (true for defaults, or object with color/width)

background.description: Background color behind text in hex format

timeRange.description: Time range for watermark visibility (video only)

fade.description: Fade in/out effect for the watermark

400.description: Validation error — invalid inputs or params

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key / session

403.description: Forbidden — job type not allowed on current plan

429.description: Rate limited — concurrent job limit or request rate exceeded

post.description: Embed an invisible tracking payload into image pixels using Meta VideoSeal 1.0. The watermark is imperceptible to humans but machine-extractable. Survives JPEG compression, resizing, rotation, and social media upload. Use watermark.detect to extract and verify the payload later.

source.description: URL to the source video or image file

payload.description: Tracking payload to embed (UUID or up to 16 bytes UTF-8)

400.description: Validation error — invalid inputs or params

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key / session

403.description: Forbidden — job type not allowed on current plan

429.description: Rate limited — concurrent job limit or request rate exceeded

post.description: Extract an invisible watermark payload from an image using Meta VideoSeal 1.0. Two modes: extract (blind — what payload is embedded?) or verify (does this image contain THIS specific payload?). Returns confidence score, bit recovery stats, and the decoded payload string.

source.description: URL to the source video or image file

expectedPayload.description: Expected payload to verify against (omit for blind extraction)

400.description: Validation error — invalid inputs or params

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key / session

403.description: Forbidden — job type not allowed on current plan

429.description: Rate limited — concurrent job limit or request rate exceeded

post.description: Generate a styled QR code image (PNG or SVG) with 9 content types: URL, WhatsApp, phone, SMS, email, vCard, WiFi, geo, and plain text. Supports branded styling with custom colors, dot styles, and logo embedding.

inputs.description: No separate inputs required — media URLs are embedded in params

url.description: URL to encode in the QR code

phone.description: Contact phone number in E.164 format

content.description: QR code content (URL, text, phone, email, vCard, WiFi, geo, SMS, or WhatsApp)

size.description: QR code image size in pixels

errorCorrection.description: Error correction level (L=7%, M=15%, Q=25%, H=30%)

backgroundColor.description: Background color in hex format or "transparent"

sizeRatio.description: Logo size relative to QR code (0.1-0.35)

margin.description: Margin around the logo in modules

post.description: Probe a media file to get duration, resolution, codecs, and stream info. Creates an extract.metadata job internally.

url.description: URL of the media file to probe

post.description: Execute a real FFmpeg command. URLs in -i positions are auto-extracted as inputs. Output format is inferred from trailing filename or set via outputFormat. Creates an FFmpeg processing job.

command.description: FFmpeg command. Can include or omit 'ffmpeg' prefix. Input files can be URLs after -i (auto-extracted) or named placeholders from inputs map. Can include output filename to set format (e.g. 'output.webm'). For standard operations (watermark, transcode, captions), prefer the built-in job types.

inputs.description: Optional named placeholder -> URL map for inputs not in the command string

post.description: Create a new media processing job. Specify the job type, input files, and processing parameters. For typed endpoints with full validation, use the category-specific routes (e.g. POST /watermark/add).

403.description: Forbidden — job type not allowed on plan

get.description: List jobs for the authenticated organization. Supports filtering by status and type, with pagination.
List all available job types with their capabilities, cost models, and required inputs.

summary.description: Short description of what the job does

mediaOverrides.description: Per-media-type overrides for output extension and cost

get.description: Retrieve details of a specific job. If the job is complete with an output, includes a download URL.

post.description: Cancel a waiting or dispatched job. Running, completed, or already cancelled jobs cannot be cancelled.

409.description: Job cannot be cancelled in current status

get.description: Stream the output file of a completed job. Only available for jobs with status 'complete' and an output file.

404.description: Job or output file not found

get.description: Retrieve execution logs for a completed job. Returns structured log entries with timestamps, levels, and step associations.

get.summary: Subscribe to job events via SSE
get.description: Establishes a Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection for real-time updates on a specific job. Works for all provider types. First event is always a job.snapshot with full current state. For terminal jobs (complete/failed/cancelled), returns a one-shot snapshot and closes.

post.summary: Create a public share link for a job output
post.description: Copies the job output to the public CDN bucket and returns a shareable URL. Requires Pro plan or higher.

400.description: Job not complete or no output

403.description: Plan does not allow sharing or quota exceeded

delete.description: Deletes the shared copy from the public bucket and purges CDN cache.

get.description: Returns the current balance in nanodollars with formatted dollar display.

rollover.description: Rollover from previous period in nanodollars

get.description: Returns aggregated usage in dollar amounts for a date range.

schema.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to first of current month.

parameters.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to first of current month.
parameters.name: start

totalMargin.description: Total margin in nanodollars (revenue minus all costs)

totalProfit.description: Alias for totalMargin — kept for backward compatibility

get.description: Returns subscription plan, status, period dates, and pending changes.
Returns tier-appropriate billing state: balance for free, balance + subscription for paid.
Returns complete information about the authenticated organization, including basic details, subscription status, plan configuration (limits, features, included usage), and current balance.

401.description: Unauthorized - missing or invalid API key

get.description: Returns all API keys for the authenticated user. Key values are never returned after creation — only metadata like name, prefix, and creation date.

post.description: Creates a new API key for the authenticated user. The full key value is returned **only once** — store it securely, as it cannot be retrieved again.

name.description: Descriptive name for the key (defaults to 'API Key')

expiresIn.description: Expiration in seconds (e.g., 2592000 = 30 days). Omit for no expiry.

key.description: Full API key value (rb_xxx...). Only returned once!

delete.description: Permanently revokes an API key. This action cannot be undone.

404.description: API key not found or already revoked

post.description: Creates a batch of jobs that will be processed together. Validates all jobs upfront, performs a single credit pre-check for the entire batch, and returns immediately with all job IDs. Use this for submitting multiple jobs efficiently.

jobs.description: Array of jobs to process as a batch

400.description: Validation error - invalid job type or params

401.description: Unauthorized - missing or invalid API key

403.description: Plan limit exceeded (batch size or job type not allowed)

get.description: Returns the current status of a batch, including total job count and completed job count. Use this to monitor batch progress. For individual job details, query each job by its ID.

401.description: Unauthorized - missing or invalid API key

post.description: Submit a pipeline definition (DAG of job nodes). The pipeline is validated, topologically sorted, and executed level-by-level. Nodes in the same level run in parallel. Outputs from upstream nodes are mapped to downstream inputs.

get.description: Retrieve the status and results of a pipeline execution.

Establishes a Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection for real-time job status updates. Keep this connection open to receive notifications when jobs complete, fail, or change status. Events are pushed immediately as they happen.

schema.description: Server-Sent Events stream. Events have format: data: {...}\n\n

401.description: Unauthorized - missing or invalid API key

post.description: Creates an asset record and returns a presigned upload URL. The client should PUT the file to the returned URL.

201.description: Asset record created with upload URL

uploadUrl.description: URL to PUT the file to

get.description: Returns a paginated list of assets for the authenticated organization. Use scope=system to list system-provided assets instead.

schema.description: Filter by scope (defaults to org assets)

parameters.description: Filter by scope (defaults to org assets)
parameters.name: scope

orgId.description: Organization ID that owns this asset

get.description: Convenience endpoint that returns system-provided PSD templates. Equivalent to GET /assets?scope=system&type=psd_template.

200.description: Paginated list of system PSD templates

orgId.description: Organization ID that owns this asset

get.description: Returns a single asset by ID. Users can access their own org's assets and system-scoped assets.

orgId.description: Organization ID that owns this asset

404.description: Asset not found or not accessible

delete.description: Deletes an asset record and its associated R2 objects. System-scoped assets cannot be deleted.

post.description: Marks an uploaded asset as ready for use. PSD templates no longer require preprocessing — Photopea handles raw PSDs directly via CF Browser Rendering.

get.description: Returns the preprocessed thumbnail PNG for a PSD template. Returns 404 if the asset has no thumbnail yet (preprocessing not complete).

put.description: Create or update the organization's webhook endpoint. Auto-generates a new signing secret on every call. The signing secret is only returned in the response body — store it securely.

rotateSecret.description: Generate a new signing secret. Set false to keep the existing secret (e.g. when only toggling active).

200.description: Webhook endpoint configured. Signing secret shown in plaintext (only time it's visible).

signingSecret.description: Plaintext signing secret — store securely, not shown again

400.description: Invalid URL (must be HTTPS, no private/internal addresses)

get.description: Returns the organization's webhook endpoint configuration. Signing secret is masked.
Returns recent webhook delivery attempts for the organization.

post.description: Sends a test event to the configured webhook endpoint and returns the delivery result.
